form.serviceIdHint: Unique identifier for the service, cannot be changed after creation
form.name: Name
form.parameterSchemaHint: JSON format, defines parameter types, ranges and constraints
form.outputConfigHint: Define the collection of output items (files or text information) after service execution
form.noOutputItems: No output items, click the button below to add
form.paramDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this parameter is used for

form.unsupportedFile: Unsupported file format. Please upload a JSON or YAML file.
form.invalidContentCannotConvert: The current content is invalid and cannot be converted.
form.baseurlHint: Base address of the service. All requests will be built under this prefix.
form.serviceSuffixHint: Suffix path used to build per-service endpoints. Supports placeholder {service_id}.
form.downloadSuffixHint: Suffix path used to build download endpoints. Supports placeholder {file_id}.
form.jsonPlaceholder:
{'{'}
  "name": "Spatial transcriptomics preprocessing",
  "description": "Preprocess and QC spatial transcriptomics data",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "baseurl": "https://api.example.com",
  "service_suffix": "/api/service",
  "download_suffix": "/api/download",
  "visibility": "private",
  "parameter_template": {'{'}
    "normalize": true,
    "qc_threshold": 0.3,
    "min_genes": 200
  {'}'}
{'}'}
form.yamlPlaceholder:
name: Spatial transcriptomics preprocessing
description: Preprocess and QC spatial transcriptomics data
version: 1.0.0
baseurl: https://api.example.com
service_suffix: /api/service
download_suffix: /api/download
visibility: private
parameter_template:
  normalize: true
  qc_threshold: 0.3
  min_genes: 200

help.title: Help Documentation
help.downloadSampleDataPrompt: Click here to download sample data

introduction.title: System Introduction
introduction.subtitle: Learn about the core features and usage of STAnalyzer

whatIs.title: What is STAnalyzer?
whatIs.description: STAnalyzer is an intelligent multi-agent framework designed to automate the end-to-end analytical lifecycle—from raw data processing to biological hypothesis generation. Transcending traditional pipelines, STAnalyzer employs a Human-in-the-Loop collaborative intelligence architecture that translates natural language queries into rigorous bioinformatics workflows, performs multimodal self-refinement, and conducts evidence-based cross-validation to bridge data-driven correlations with biological causation.

projectManagement.title: Project & Data Management
projectManagement.description: Organize files, execution records, and results by project, supporting parallel management of multiple projects

dagVisualization.title: Execution DAG Visualization
dagVisualization.description: Automatically generate and display execution graphs (DAG), showing files, execution steps, and their dependencies

multiPerspective.title: Multi-perspective Data Analysis
multiPerspective.description: Support file perspective, execution perspective, and visualization analysis for specific data types

intelligentAgent.title: Intelligent Agent Dialogue
intelligentAgent.description: Interact with Agent through natural language, automatically generate analysis plans, execute services, and explain results

serviceManagement.title: Service Management
serviceManagement.description: Provide service information queries and relationship graphs to help understand the support relationships between services and file types

automation.title: Automation
automation.description: Automatically execute complex analysis workflows through intelligent Agent, reducing manual operations

integration.title: Integration
integration.description: Integrate project management, data management, visualization, and intelligent dialogue in one platform

intelligent.title: Intelligence
intelligent.description: Interact with Agent through natural language, automatically generate analysis plans and explain results

visualization.title: Visualization
visualization.description: Rich visualization components supporting multiple data types and analysis perspectives

systemIdentity.title: System Identity
systemIdentity.description: STAnalyzer (Spatial Transcriptomics Analyzer by Multi-agent Analysis System)

primaryFunction.title: Primary Function
primaryFunction.description: End-to-end automated analysis lifecycle for Spatial Transcriptomics (ST) data

mission.title: Mission
mission.description: Bridge the gap between complex raw data and verifiable biological insights by eliminating manual coordination bottlenecks

intentDriven.title: Intent-Driven Orchestration
intentDriven.description: Natural Language Interface (NLI) converts user queries into rigorous bioinformatics workflows

multiModal.title: Multi-Modal Self-Optimization
multiModal.description: Closed-loop validation combining visual patterns (e.g., UMAP) and statistical metrics

evidenceBased.title: Evidence-Based Cross-Validation
evidenceBased.description: Anchors data-driven correlations in causal biological explanations via literature and structured databases (UniProt, PubMed)

fullTraceability.title: Full Traceability
fullTraceability.description: DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) visualization of execution flow and data lineage

orchestrator.title: Orchestrator Agent (OA)
orchestrator.description: User-centric coordinator translating natural language requests into structured actions while maintaining global analytical consistency.

servicePlanner.title: Service Planner Agent (SPA)
servicePlanner.description: Addresses bioinformatics complexity in tool matching, configuration, and execution via a robust closed-loop containerized framework.

dataInterpretation.title: Data Interpretation Agent (DIA)
dataInterpretation.description: Multi-modal understanding of text, numerical matrices, and images to transform heterogeneous outputs into biological conclusions.

knowledgeIntegration.title: Knowledge Integration Agent (KIA)
knowledgeIntegration.description: Bridges the gap between data correlations and biological causations using external databases and literature retrieval (RAG).

interactiveVisualization.title: Interactive Visualization
interactiveVisualization.description: Tree-structured workflow display, Full-screen image preview, and Interactive data lists

serviceManagement.title: Service Management
serviceManagement.description: "Private/Public/System" service classification, Real-time status synchronization, and Parameter validation

executionMonitoring.title: Execution Monitoring
executionMonitoring.description: Comprehensive log records, State tracking (Success/Failure), and Audit trails (User ID, Timestamps)

lowBarrier.title: Low Technical Barrier
lowBarrier.description: Democratizes access to complex ST data analysis

highEfficiency.title: High Efficiency
highEfficiency.description: Accelerates the conversion of data into discoveries

reproducibility.title: Reproducibility
reproducibility.description: Persistent project saving and full input/output archiving (via UUID)

gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
gettingStarted.description: Completing a complete analysis workflow with STAnalyzer typically includes the following steps
gettingStarted.transitionText: Next, let's further communicate with AI. Taking the following steps as an example, experience more powerful functions through Prompt!

createProject.title: Create Project
createProject.description: Projects are the basic unit for organizing analysis data and execution records. Each project has its own file list, DAG view, and Agent conversation context.
createProject.point1: Click the New Project button in the left project list
createProject.point3: Click Save to create the project
createProject.tip: Projects are the basic unit for organizing analysis data and execution records

uploadData.title: Upload Data
uploadData.description: Uploading data files is a prerequisite for analysis. The system supports multiple file formats, including spatial transcriptomics data, text files, configuration files, etc.
uploadData.point2: Click the Add Data button to open the upload dialog
uploadData.point_3: Fill in file information and select the correct file type
uploadData.point3: Select a file to upload, and the system will automatically match the file type and the file name
uploadData.point4: Click Upload to upload the file
uploadData.tip: Correct file type selection is crucial for subsequent analysis

addContext.title: Add to Context
addContext.description: Adding key files to context is an important technique to improve Agent analysis quality. By providing relevant files as context, the Agent can more comprehensively understand the project background and task requirements.
addContext.point1: Select files in the DAG panel
addContext.point2: Click Add to Context to add files
addContext.point3: The Agent will reference these files in subsequent conversations
addContext.tip: Adding to context helps the Agent better understand the project background

askAgent.title: Chat with Agent
askAgent.description: Through natural language dialogue with the Agent, complex analysis tasks can be automatically completed. This is the core functionality of STAnalyzer.
askAgent.point1: Enter your analysis requirements in natural language
askAgent.point2: The Agent will automatically generate and execute the analysis plan
askAgent.point3: View analysis results and reports in the conversation panel
askAgent.tip: The more detailed the query, the more accurate the Agent's response

viewReport.title: View Report
viewReport.description: After analysis completes, the Agent will output an analysis report in the conversation panel
viewReport.tip: Reports support Markdown format for easy reading

analysis.title: Analysis of Downstream Applications
analysis.description: Based on the preprocessed data, perform downstream application analysis, such as Spatial Transcriptomics Processing. Here, we use Spatial Transcriptomics Processing performed on the preprocessed_data.h5ad file as an example.
analysis.point1: Left-click the node, then select “Spatial Transcriptomics Processing” under “Further Analysis” in the list
analysis.point2: After entering the parameters, click “Execute Service”
analysis.point3: Click each output node to view the execution results
analysis.tip: This process, known as manual service execution, is the second method of execution besides “chat with agent.”

analysis2.title: Perform more in-depth analysis
analysis2.description: Conduct detailed cell type composition analysis and gene enrichment analysis for each of the 9 spatial domains, and summarize results in a tabular format.
analysis2.prompt: Please analyze the main cell type composition of each of the 9 spatial domains one by one（Each spatial domain contains more than one type of cell, and it is composed of multiple cell types）, and perform gene enrichment analysis for each domain. Finally, summarize the analysis results for each spatial domain in tabular form.
analysis2.tip: Comprehensive analysis of each spatial domain provides deeper insights into cellular heterogeneity.

illustrations.title: Additional scientific illustrations
illustrations.description: Create scientific visualizations showing cell type composition and enriched terms for each spatial domain.
illustrations.prompt: Based on this result, draw the composition of cell types in each spatial domain (only the top 3 cell types need to be shown for each spatial domain) as well as the enriched terms. Please ensure that the appropriate scientific research charts are presented in a clear and understandable manner.
illustrations.tip: Clear visualizations help in better understanding complex spatial relationships and patterns.

validation.title: Analysis and Validation
validation.description: Analyze and infer functional domains for each spatial region based on cell type annotations and enrichment results, providing explainable evidence.
validation.prompt: Based on the cell type annotations and gene enrichment analysis results of these 9 spatial domains that have been completed, please further analyze and infer the functional domains that each spatial domain may belong to (provide explainable evidence), such as the stromal region, tumor occurrence area, etc. Finally, form a summary table to clearly display the characteristics of each spatial domain.
validation.tip: Providing explainable evidence strengthens the validity and reliability of functional domain inferences.

researchDirections.title: Explore Valuable Research Directions
researchDirections.description: Identify significant research directions and cell communication patterns among spatial domains to guide biological discoveries.
researchDirections.prompt: Utilize the platform to explore valuable research directions, guide meaningful biological discoveries, and avoid blind large-scale analysis. Perform cell communication analysis among the 9 spatial domains, identify the two spatial domains with the greatest research significance and the cell interactions between them, and provide an explanatory description as well as a summary report.
researchDirections.tip: Focusing on cell communication patterns can reveal critical biological interactions and research opportunities.

multiAgent.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration for Accelerated Biological Discoveries
multiAgent.description: Leverage multi-agent collaboration to analyze subcellular interactions at the boundary between adaptive immunity and immunosuppressive domains.
multiAgent.prompt: The current dataset possesses a truly accurate resolution of subcellular physical coordinates. Our previous analysis has successfully identified a crucial physical communication boundary: Domain 0 (the core of adaptive immunity dominated by T cells) and Domain 3 (the immunosuppressive barrier dominated by Tregs). Please focus on the interface where physical contact occurs between Domain 0 and Domain 3 (the boundary). From the perspective of subcellular analysis, deduce and extract potential new biological discoveries. Please conduct a deep analysis using an explainable thought chain (Chain of Thought).
multiAgent.tip: Multi-agent collaboration enables more comprehensive and explainable biological discoveries at subcellular resolution.

automaticCodeAnalysis.title: Automatic Code Analysis
automaticCodeAnalysis.description: Automatically analyze the code to extract key functional domains and cell interactions.

mechanismBasedReasoning.title: Mechanism-Based Reasoning
mechanismBasedReasoning.description: Synthesize all available evidence from file metadata, decision history, and established knowledge base to automatically propose deep, mechanism-based reasoning results (each hypothesis has a traceable chain of thought):

summaryReport.title: Summary Report
summaryReport.description: Provide a summary report of the novel biological phenomena discovered:

analysisPage.title: Analysis Page
analysisPage.description: The Analysis page is the core working area of STAnalyzer, integrating project management, data visualization, execution workflow management, and intelligent dialogue
analysisPage.transitionText: Next, we will introduce the analysis features that enable you to perform comprehensive data analysis and visualization.

leftPanel.title: Left Panel
leftPanel.description: Entry point for project and data management

projectList.title: Project List
projectList.feature2: Support project selection, deletion, and preview
projectList.feature3: Quick access to project DAG view

fileList.title: File List
fileList.feature1: Display all files under the current project
fileList.feature2: Support file upload, edit, and delete operations

middleContent.title: Middle Content Area
middleContent.description: Core display area of the Analysis page

dagView.title: DAG Visualization
dagView.description: By default, displays the current project's execution graph (DAG - Directed Acyclic Graph)

elements.nodesDetail1: Execution step nodes: Represent service calls or analysis operations
elements.nodesDetail2: File nodes: Represent input or output data files
elements.edgesDetail1: Arrow direction represents data flow direction
elements.edgesDetail2: Edge styles may represent different relationship types
elements.filesDesc: Each node can associate multiple files, displaying detailed file information

dataVisualization.title: Data Visualization
dataVisualization.feature1: Automatically switch to corresponding visualization component based on file type
dataVisualization.feature2: Support multiple visualization types: tables, charts, interactive graphics, etc.
dataVisualization.feature3: Click the file again to return to DAG view

rightPanel.title: Right Agent Panel
rightPanel.description: Core interaction area for intelligent analysis
rightPanel.feature1: Natural language dialogue with multi-agent system
rightPanel.feature2: Automatically generate analysis plans and explanations based on project context
rightPanel.feature3: Support adding files to context and triggering specific analysis components

naturalLanguageExecution.title: Natural Language Execution
naturalLanguageExecution.description: Using Agent for natural language dialogue, users can input analysis questions or requests in plain language, and the system will automatically generate analysis plans and explanations based on project context

manualExecution.title: Manual Service Execution
manualExecution.description: In addition to using Agent for automatic analysis, the system also supports manual service execution, giving users more control

step1.title: Open Manual Execution Page
step1.description: In DAG view or file list, find the service to execute and click the execute button

step2.title: Fill Execution Parameters
step2.description: The system will automatically fill in the file ID and validate whether the file type matches service requirements

step3.title: Execute Service
step3.description: Click the Execute button to start executing the service, and view execution logs

step4.title: View Results
step4.description: After execution completes, the system will automatically refresh the DAG view, and newly generated result files will be added to the file list

serviceManagement.title: Service Management
serviceManagement.description: STAnalyzer provides a definitive hub for service registration, orchestration, and visual management. It enables researchers to intuitively orchestrate robust data analysis pipelines while empowering developers to seamlessly integrate their own custom bioinformatics algorithms.

serviceInfo.title: Service Information Query
serviceInfo.description: The Service Information Query page provides detailed information about all available services in the system

functions.title: Function Description
functions.function1: List all available analysis services in the system
functions.function2: Display service details: name, description, input/output file types, parameters, etc.
functions.function3: Support search and filtering to quickly find specific services

useCases.title: Use Cases
useCases.case1: Plan analysis workflow: Before uploading new type data, confirm whether there are suitable processing services
useCases.case2: Understand service capabilities: When planning the entire analysis workflow, understand which services can be called at each step
useCases.case3: Parameter configuration: View parameters supported by services, prepare for manual service execution

relationshipGraph.title: Relationship Graph Query
relationshipGraph.description: Relationship Graph Query displays services, file types, and their relationships in a visual way

components.title: Relationship Graph Components
components.component1: Service nodes: Represent various analysis services in the system
components.component2: File type nodes: Represent various file types supported by the system
components.component3: Relationship edges: Connect service nodes and file type nodes, representing input/output/support relationships

interactions.title: Interactive Functions
interactions.interaction1: Click nodes to highlight all related connections
interactions.interaction2: Track complete paths from one file type to another file type
interactions.interaction3: View which file types a service depends on, or which services can process a certain file type

useCases.title: Use Cases
useCases.case1: Workflow planning: Understand how data flows between services from a global perspective
useCases.case2: Dependency analysis: Understand what prerequisites a service needs, or what subsequent analyses a file type can be used for
useCases.case3: Bottleneck identification: Discover potential workflow bottlenecks or service components that need to be supplemented

serviceUpload.title: Service Upload & Registration Guide
serviceUpload.description: Learn how to integrate and register your own analysis algorithms into STAnalyzer.

downloadBanner.title: Example Service Template Download
downloadBanner.description: To help you quickly understand the service configuration structure, download the example PASTE 3D Alignment Service package.

step1.title: Create New Service
step1.desc: Go to Service Management and click "Create Service" in the top right corner.

step2.title: Basic Information
step2.desc: Fill in the service name, description, version, and set visibility.

step3.title: Configure API
step3.desc: Set the remote server Base URL and the execution/download endpoint suffixes.

step4.title: Input Parameters
step4.desc: Define required parameters (e.g. thresholds, methods) and their constraints.

step5.title: Required Files
step5.desc: Specify the input files required by the algorithm (e.g. h5ad matrices or images).

step6.title: Output Configuration
step6.desc: Declare the result files or text that your service will output.

step7.title: Import Config
step7.desc: Optionally upload a service_config.json file to auto-fill all configurations.

executionManagement.title: Execution Management
executionManagement.description: The Execution Management page is the center for viewing and managing all execution records, providing a complete view of execution history

executionList.title: Execution List View
executionList.feature1: Display all execution records in table form
executionList.feature2: Support filtering by project, time, status, service, and other dimensions
executionList.feature3: View execution ID, status, time, duration, and other information
executionList.feature4: Support combined filtering, can use multiple filter conditions simultaneously

executionDetails.title: Execution Details View
executionDetails.feature1: View execution parameters and input files
executionDetails.feature2: View all output files generated by the execution
executionDetails.feature4: Highlight the execution's path in the DAG

status.label: Status
status.value: Execution status: success, failure, running, waiting

time.label: Time
time.value: Start time, end time, and duration

project.selectProjectPrompt: Please select a project to start analysis
project.name: Project Name
project.deleteProjectConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this project? This action cannot be undone.

actions.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete project "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.

knowledgeSearch.title: Knowledge Search
knowledgeSearch.queryPlaceholder: Enter your query statement, e.g., What are the latest advances in machine learning?
knowledgeSearch.queryMinLength: Query content must be at least 1 character
knowledgeSearch.sourcesHint: Optional: PubMed, ArXiv, CrossRef, leave empty to use all
knowledgeSearch.rewriteHint: Enable to automatically optimize query statements
knowledgeSearch.traceHint: Enable to return detailed debug information

spatialCoexpression.title: Spatial Coexpression Network Visualization
spatialCoexpression.subtitle: Integrated analysis tool for gene expression networks and spatial distribution

spatialTranscriptomics.title: Spatial Transcriptomics Data Analysis Platform
spatialTranscriptomics.subtitle: Clustering and visualization tool for spatial transcriptomics data
spatialTranscriptomics.adjustParamsHint: Use the left control panel to adjust parameters

cellComm.subtitle: Ligand-Receptor Interaction Visualization
cellComm.networkHint: Nodes: cells; Edges: ligand-receptor interactions, width ~ normalized score
